prerequisites: A CoinAPI account. Sign up at coinapi.io to access the customer dashboard.
A CoinAPI API key generated from the dashboard, supplied as the api_key secret. The connector sends it in the X-CoinAPI-Key request header.
A target environment: set environment to production (https://rest.coinapi.io) or sandbox (https://rest-sandbox.coinapi.io).
A symbol_id identifying the market data series to read, for example BITSTAMP_SPOT_BTC_USD.
A period (period_id) such as 1DAY when syncing OHLCV historical data, plus a start_date defining the lower time bound of the backfill.

authMethods.name: API key (X-CoinAPI-Key header)
authMethods.summary: CoinAPI authenticates every REST request with a single API key. Provide it as the api_key secret; the connector passes it in the X-CoinAPI-Key HTTP request header. Generate the key from the CoinAPI customer dashboard after signing up. The same key works against both the production host (rest.coinapi.io) and the sandbox host (rest-sandbox.coinapi.io).

setupSteps.title: Create a CoinAPI account and generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Sign up at coinapi.io and open the customer dashboard. Generate an API key. Copy the key value; it is used as the api_key secret and is sent in the X-CoinAPI-Key request header on every call.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key secret
setupSteps.body: Set the required, secret api_key field to the API key from the dashboard. The connector uses this key to authenticate requests.

setupSteps.title: Select the environment
setupSteps.body: Set the required environment field to production to target https://rest.coinapi.io, or sandbox to target https://rest-sandbox.coinapi.io.

setupSteps.title: Configure the symbol and history window
setupSteps.body: Set the required symbol_id (for example BITSTAMP_SPOT_BTC_USD) and required start_date that bounds the start of the historical backfill. For OHLCV historical data, also set the required period (period_id) such as 1DAY. Optionally set end_date to cap the upper time bound and limit to control page size.
